title: 5-glyph Latin pilot sheet, sourced + reviewed, end-to-end
context: The M0 milestone must prove the complete data-first loop — model → renderer → accessible PDF → review — on a minimal real unit before scaling. A 5-glyph Latin pilot (e.g. a c o l 1) exercises every layer: schema-valid glyph models, gate-verified font outlines, deterministic SVG render with 4-line guidelines, PDF output in A4 and Letter, and reviewer sign-off on formation accuracy. This is the cold-start thin slice that validates the whole system works before committing to the full A–Z model.
objective: Model 5 Latin glyphs (e.g. a, c, o, l, 1) against the schema-001 schema, render a trace sheet in A4 and US Letter using the gate-verified font, have the formation reviewer sign the per-glyph checklist, and publish the CC-BY-4.0 PDFs + model to the repo.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 Latin glyphs (e.g. a c o l 1) modeled as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef citing an authoritative formation source.
Glyph outlines sourced exclusively from the gate-verified open font (font-003 PASS artifact attached).
Renderer produces a deterministic trace sheet in both A4 and US Letter with a 4-line/3-zone guideline system; golden-file SVG test passes in CI.
Formation reviewer signs the per-glyph checklist confirming stroke order and direction match the cited source.
PDFs + model committed CC-BY-4.0; every sheet footer carries font attribution and license text.
Output handed to an educator from outreach-008 if available; otherwise published to repo with verifiedNeed:false and adoption blocker explicitly surfaced in a BLOCKERS note.
resources: PLAN.md section 9 M0 (cold-start thin slice, 5-glyph pilot)
gate-002 (provenance gate policy and PASS artifact)
output: 5-glyph Latin pilot model (data/latin/pilot/), trace sheet PDFs (A4 + Letter), golden-file SVG fixtures, reviewer-signed per-glyph checklist, and gate PASS artifact — all committed to the repo with CC-BY-4.0 licensing.
